text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by analyzing huge amounts of written material, such as books, articles, and websites, which is called training data. From this data, the model learns patterns in language, like grammar, facts, and even style. When you give an LLM a prompt, such as a question or instruction, it predicts the next words based on what it has learned. This process, called prompt engineering, helps users guide the model to get useful answers. LLMs can write essays, summarize information, or hold conversations. However, they have limits. They might invent incorrect facts, lack real understanding, and cannot think critically or creatively. So, while powerful, they need careful use.
